scope: Verified Unity-owned generated geometry component for route-safe valve wheel wall console dressing.
constraints: Implementation is owned by the main Unity integration lane.
The promoted component remains visual-only route dressing.
Do not introduce gameplay authority, puzzle state, input handling, runtime interaction logic, or build changes through this component.
Prototype direction must remain Unity-only: primitives, procedural meshes, project-native materials, and Unity test renders.

north_star.primary_affordance: Large forward-facing valve wheel that reads as operable without adding gameplay behavior.

allowed_methods: procedural elbows or segmented cylinder pipe approximations
project-native decals, vertex colors, or material masks for grime
major_parts: amber pilot lamp bezels and lenses
oil streaks, soot patches, and edge wear

material_targets.name: aged_brass
material_targets.intent: Warm tarnished brass with polished contact wear on rims, spokes, rivets, and corners.

material_targets.name: blackened_iron
material_targets.intent: Charcoal iron with soot-dark recesses, chipped edges, and subtle brown oxidation.

material_targets.name: amber_lamp_glass
material_targets.intent: Controlled warm emissive amber lens that reads as a pilot lamp without clipping detail.

material_targets.name: gauge_glass
material_targets.intent: Subtle clear glossy cover that preserves face and needle readability.

material_targets.name: oil_grime
material_targets.intent: Dark glossy grime placed under axle points, pipe joints, lower edges, and panel seams.

acceptance_criteria: Console can be built in Unity from primitives, procedural meshes, project-native materials, and optional project-native decals. Status: passed.
Large valve wheel is the primary visual affordance and remains readable from normal gameplay distance. Status: passed.
Required visual elements are present: brass/blackened iron console, valve wheel, gauges, amber lamps, pipes, rivets, and oil grime. Status: passed.
No gameplay authority, puzzle state, input handling, runtime interaction logic, objective logic, or build/release changes are introduced. Status: passed.
Materials clearly distinguish brass, blackened iron, glass, amber emission, and grime. Status: passed.
Gauges and lamps imply future state without presenting authoritative live values. Status: passed.
Unity validation and full V0 matrix show no missing required hierarchy/material/count/route-safety gates. Status: passed.
validation_checklist: Review all changed files and confirm they are inside the target documentation folder.
Confirm the production brief includes acceptance criteria, Unity primitive/procedural mesh approach, material targets, validation checklist, and Unity test render evaluation guidance.
Confirm the brief preserves the docs-only boundary and avoids gameplay implementation authority.

test_render_evaluation.environment: Unity Game view or Scene view using project rendering.
fail_conditions: Valve wheel is not the strongest affordance.
Brass reads as plastic or bright gold.
Blackened iron and grime collapse into one dark mass.
Amber lamps bloom enough to erase nearby detail.
Gauge faces cannot be recognized as pressure gauges.
Pipes, rivets, or brackets appear detached.
Asset implies active gameplay state before gameplay integration exists.
